    {E : Type*} [SeminormedAddCommGroup E]

          (fun n : ℕ =>

node.natural_language_proof:
Proof (root test): let `L` be the limsup of `‖a (n+1)‖^(1/(n+1))`. Choose `r` with `L < r < 1`.
Then for all sufficiently large `n` we have `‖a (n+1)‖^(1/(n+1)) ≤ r`, hence
`‖a (n+1)‖ ≤ r^(n+1)`. Therefore the tail of `∑ ‖a n‖` is bounded by a geometric series with ratio
`r`, which converges. Hence `∑ ‖a n‖` is summable.
